summary: Finished matching MetroTRK by converting the remaining MetroTRK/msghndlr.c and MetroTRK/dolphin_trk.c objects from NonMatching to Matching, with targeted source fixes in message handling, target address translation, and public headers. The PR also added a compiler warning suppression for sometimes-uninitialized diagnostics needed by the matched source shape.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so MetroTRK/msghndlr.c and MetroTRK/dolphin_trk.c are built as Matching instead of NonMatching.
Added -Wno-sometimes-uninitialized to compile_flags.txt, apparently to tolerate warning-prone matched control/data flow.
Updated dolphin_trk.c to include its own MetroTRK/dolphin_trk.h header instead of only platform.h.

Added a special-case path in TRKTargetTranslate: addresses in the MTRK_NubInit_804A50C8 range of size 0x4000 are returned unchanged when gTRKCPUState.Extended1.DBAT3U has the low BAT validity bits set; other addresses still map through BOOTINFO.
Expanded dolphin_trk.h declarations for __TRK_reset, InitMetroTRK, TRKTargetTranslate, and TRKInitializeTarget, and included MetroTRK/dserror.h for DSError.
Expanded msghndlr.h declarations for TRKMessageIntoReply, TRKDoUnsupported, TRKDoCPUType, and TRKDoFlushCache.

In msghndlr.c, added explicit DSError-to-DSReplyError mappings for kInvalidProcessId, kInvalidThreadId, and kOsError in several handlers instead of falling through to generic kDSReplyCWDSError or kDSReplyError.
Removed early TRKTargetStopped checks from TRKDoReadRegisters, TRKDoWriteRegisters, and TRKDoFlushCache in the shown diff, changing the order of validation/control flow to match target code.
Moved the TRKTargetStopped check in TRKDoStep later, after step option validation and unsupported-option handling.
Reworked TRKDoStop from a simple success/error conditional into a switch over TRKTargetStop results, preserving specific reply errors for invalid process ID, invalid thread ID, and OS error.
smart_moves: Kept the matching transition narrow: only the two remaining MetroTRK objects were flipped to Matching after localized source/header/build changes.
Matched original error-reply behavior by preserving specific reply codes rather than collapsing target errors to generic debugger errors.

Adjusted validation order instead of only adding logic, which is often necessary for MetroTRK/message-handler matching because emitted control flow depends heavily on early returns and switch placement.
Added prototypes to the relevant MetroTRK headers so source files can include their own module headers, improving declaration consistency while supporting matching.
Used the BAT/DBAT3U guard in TRKTargetTranslate to model an address-translation exception without disturbing the default BOOTINFO-based translation path.
decomp_lessons: When finishing a subsystem, verify configure.py object status; source can be effectively matched only when the object is switched from NonMatching to Matching.
For MetroTRK handlers, exact switch cases for DSError-to-DSReplyError conversion matter for matching and should not be simplified to broad defaults.
The order of protocol validation checks can be as important as the checks themselves; moving TRKTargetStopped later can change codegen/control flow enough to match.
Headers should expose real cross-file entry points once a module is matched, especially when replacing generic platform includes with the module's own header.
Address translation code may need hardware-state conditionals such as DBAT validity checks; avoid over-generalizing all addresses through BOOTINFO if the binary has mapped-range exceptions.
Compiler warning suppressions in compile_flags.txt can be part of preserving source shape for matching, but should be tied to concrete matched code rather than broad refactors.
naming_conventions: MetroTRK function names use TRK-prefixed CamelCase such as TRKTargetTranslate, TRKInitializeTarget, TRKDoFlushCache, and TRKMessageIntoReply.
MetroTRK error enums use k-prefixed names such as kNoError, kInvalidProcessId, kInvalidThreadId, kOsError, kDSReplyInvalidProcessId, and kDSReplyNoError.
Initialization/reset symbols retain original-style names including SECTION_INIT void __TRK_reset(void) and InitMetroTRK.
The message buffer typedef is named msgbuf_t while many function signatures use MessageBuffer* directly.
The global CPU state symbol appears as gTRKCPUState, with nested hardware register fields such as Extended1.DBAT3U.
The special nub/init address symbol appears as MTRK_NubInit_804A50C8, preserving an address-suffixed decomp naming style for unidentified or address-anchored data/code.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Convert NonMatching objects to Matching only after resolving remaining source-level control-flow and declaration differences.
Use explicit switch statements over error enums to reproduce binary branch tables or compare chains rather than relying on compact if/default logic.
Preserve validation order: for TRKDoStep, unsupported step options are handled before the stopped-target check in the matched source.
Avoid generic early returns when the target binary performs additional argument validation first; this was reflected by removing early stopped checks from register and cache handlers.
Add narrow hardware-state guards, such as DBAT3U low-bit checks and fixed address-window comparisons, to match target address-translation behavior.
Include module headers from their implementation files to force prototype consistency and reduce implicit or mismatched declarations that can perturb compilation.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided excerpt.
The PR context reports one issue comment but provides no human text excerpt, so no reviewer intent or discussion can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching MetroTRK/msghndlr.c to see whether these error mappings or stopped-target check ordering were refined.
Search for MTRK_NubInit_804A50C8 and DBAT3U usage to understand the broader MetroTRK memory translation model.
Audit compile_flags.txt history for -Wno-sometimes-uninitialized to see whether it was introduced solely for MetroTRK matching or reused by other matched objects.
Compare other TRKDo* handlers for consistent DSError-to-DSReplyError switch patterns.
